explanation: The original Sigma rule specifically monitors for redirection operators ('>') used within the Command Prompt environment (cmd.exe) targeting %TEMP% or %TMP%. By executing the reconnaissance command through PowerShell and using the 'Out-File' cmdlet or the 'Set-Content' cmdlet instead of the standard redirector, the 'selection_redirect' condition is not met.

The rule is strictly limited to files being redirected into the %TEMP% or %TMP% directories. By simply changing the output destination to any other writeable directory, such as C:\Users\Public\ or the user's home directory, the 'selection_redirect' filter fails to trigger while the reconnaissance activity remains successful.

The Sigma rule looks for ' > ' (a single greater-than sign with surrounding spaces). In many shell environments, using the append operator '>>' without the specific spacing expected by the string match (or using it without leading spaces) can bypass simple string-based 'contains' filters while still achieving the goal of exporting data to the temp folder.
